preview_text:
The model favors Belgium at 65%, leaving New Zealand a modest 11% chance of victory. The underdog sits well below even a coin-flip scenario, with the gap between the two teams' win probabilities spanning more than 50 percentage points on the model's reading.

The 24% draw probability suggests a moderate but genuine possibility of a stalemate. Belgium's win likelihood dominates the outcome space, yet the draw sits meaningfully above the underdog's win chance, indicating the match retains competitive structure rather than appearing one-sided by the full spread alone. A Belgium victory remains the clear modal outcome, but the model does not project a rout.

The market consensus strips 9% from New Zealand's chances and adds roughly 9% to Belgium's win probability compared to the model. The draw falls by 7 percentage points in sportsbook consensus. These gaps exceed 5 points on each outcome, signaling material divergence: the market prices Belgium materially shorter than the model does. The dashboard makes no claim about which source better calibrates the true state of play.
